english_translation: Those who give food give life itself.
author: Cittalai Cattanar
commentary: Food sustains life, and therefore feeding the hungry is the ultimate virtue.

english_translation: Only those who walk the path of virtue attain a peaceful mind.
author: Cittalai Cattanar
translator_notes: Manimekalai's Buddhist ethic — virtue (Aram/Dharma) leads to mental peace.

tamil_text: கல்வி கற்றோரே உண்மையான செல்வர் அல்லர் பொருள் உடையோரே.
english_translation: Those who have gained learning are the truly wealthy, not those who merely have material possessions.
author: Cittalai Cattanar
translator_notes: The Manimekalai's elevation of learning over material wealth as the measure of true richness.

english_translation: To rejoice in the happiness of others is the highest virtue.
author: Cittalai Cattanar
translator_notes: Universal compassion (Arul) expressed as the capacity to feel joy in others' happiness.

tamil_text: அன்னை ஆவுடையார் தன் வாழ்க்கையை அறத்திற்கு அர்ப்பணித்தாள்.
english_translation: Auvudaiyar dedicated her life entirely to virtue (Aram).
author: Cittalai Cattanar
translator_notes: The heroine's renunciation and dedication to virtue mirrors the Buddhist path of the text.

tamil_text: மாணிக்கவாசகர் கொடை உலகம் புகழும் திறன் உடையது.
english_translation: The generosity of Manikkavacakar is capable of winning the praise of the whole world.
author: Cittalai Cattanar
translator_notes: Generosity (Igai) as the path to eternal fame (Pukazh) in the Manimekalai tradition.
